page.content: In the FY2023 report by China State Construction regarding Construction & Building Materials, the table detailing project statistics reveals that Residential Projects have a BIM Adoption Rate of 85%, with an Investment Amount of £15M, yielding Cost Savings of £3M, and are marked as Completed with a Completion Date of Q42023. Commercial Projects show a BIM Adoption Rate of 75%, an Investment Amount of £20M, Cost Savings of £5M, are Ongoing, and will conclude in Q22024. Infrastructure Projects have a high BIM Adoption Rate of 90%, with an Investment Amount of £12M and Cost Savings of £2M, also Completed with a Completion Date of Q12023. Mixed-Use Projects exhibit a BIM Adoption Rate of 70%, an Investment Amount of £18M, and Cost Savings of £4M, remain Ongoing, projected for Q32024.

lines.text: In the FY2023 report by China State Construction regarding Construction & Building Materials, the table detailing project
statistics reveals that Residential Projects have a BIM Adoption Rate of 85%, with an Investment Amount of £15M, yielding
Cost Savings of £3M, and are marked as Completed with a Completion Date of Q42023. Commercial Projects show a BIM
Adoption Rate of 75%, an Investment Amount of £20M, Cost Savings of £5M, are Ongoing, and will conclude in Q22024.
Infrastructure Projects have a high BIM Adoption Rate of 90%, with an Investment Amount of £12M and Cost Savings of £2M,
also Completed with a Completion Date of Q12023. Mixed-Use Projects exhibit a BIM Adoption Rate of 70%, an Investment
Amount of £18M, and Cost Savings of £4M, remain Ongoing, projected for Q32024.

page.content: Total Projects have an 80% BIM Adoption Rate, with a cumulative Investment Amount of £65M and Cost Savings of £14M, labeled as Varied with no specified Completion Date. Additionally, BIM Training achieved a 100% Adoption Rate with £1M invested, Completed in Q12023, while the Feedback Score stands at 4.5/5, and Future Projects expect an 85% Adoption Rate with £30M allocated and £6M in Cost Savings, Planned for Q42024.

lines.text: Total Projects have an 80% BIM Adoption Rate, with a cumulative Investment Amount of £65M and Cost Savings of £14M,
labeled as Varied with no specified Completion Date. Additionally, BIM Training achieved a 100% Adoption Rate with £1M
invested, Completed in Q12023, while the Feedback Score stands at 4.5/5, and Future Projects expect an 85% Adoption Rate
with £30M allocated and £6M in Cost Savings, Planned for Q42024.

page.content: In the Infrastructure Spending table, the Allocation Breakdown shows Road Construction at £45M with a YoY Change of 5.2%, Bridge Development at £30M with a YoY Change of 4.8%, Rail Projects at £25M with a YoY Change of 6.1%, Utilities at £15M with a YoY Change of 3.5%, culminating in a Total Investment of £115M, reflecting Market Share(%).
